title: Fix: Prevent error in lldb `kernel vmmap`
body:
The `kernel vmmap` command could raise an error in LLDB due to `kernel_vmmap()` not being fully compatible with the LLDB environment. Specifically, when `get_known_pages()` returned no pages, `LLDBProcess.vmmap()` would still attempt to call `kernel_vmmap()`, leading to an error when `KernelVmmap.adjust` tried to iterate over potentially invalid LLDBValue objects.

This commit modifies `LLDBProcess.vmmap()` to return an empty `LLDBMemoryMap` directly if `get_known_pages()` yields no results, thus avoiding the problematic call path in LLDB.

A new test has been added in `tests/lldb-tests/tests/test_command_vmmap.py` to verify that the `vmmap` command executes without error in LLDB and handles cases with no memory mappings gracefully.

Fixes #3102

step_plan.summary: Prevent LLDB `kernel vmmap`/`vmmap` from erroring when LLDB reports no memory regions by making LLDBProcess.vmmap return an empty memory map instead of falling back to the kernel vmmap provider. Add an LLDB-focused test to ensure the vmmap command succeeds and handles the empty-map case gracefully.

steps.operation: Add an early-return guard in the LLDB vmmap implementation so it never falls back to kernel_vmmap when LLDB reports no known pages
steps.reason: In LLDB, the current fallback path can call pwndbg.aglib.kernel.vmmap.kernel_vmmap(), which performs adjustments that assume debugger/value semantics that may not hold under LLDB. When get_known_pages() returns an empty list, returning an empty LLDBMemoryMap is the correct and safest behavior and avoids the incompatible kernel vmmap code path entirely.
steps.side_effects: Behavior change is LLDB-only: vmmap output may become empty (instead of attempting a kernel fallback) when LLDB provides no regions. This should prevent runtime exceptions in LLDB and should not affect GDB/QEMU backends. Any callers expecting a kernel-derived map in LLDB when regions are empty will now receive an empty map, so command output will be minimal but stable.
